<findings>

[observation] [info] Isolated files — no import edges between 4 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0 is expected for standalone scripts with no inter-file imports; no action needed unless the script count grows significantly.
[observation] [info] Gateway validation scripts (check-gateway-regex.mjs, check-gateway-test.mjs) duplicate enforcement that could live in CI — confirm these are wired into the CI pipeline to guarantee they run on every PR.
[observation] [info] Scripts use .mjs extension while most source is TypeScript — consider whether type-safe TypeScript scripts would catch more issues at author-time.
[pattern] [info] Scripts zone is a pure leaf in the dependency graph (coupling 0, no imports from other zones) — this property should be preserved; any future shared utility pulled in from a package would create an implicit build-time dependency on that package.
[relationship] [warning] monorepo-maintenance-scripts enforces gateway discipline for hench and web but has no equivalent check for the rex or sourcevision packages, leaving those gateways unguarded by static script validation.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Maintenance scripts use .mjs (untyped) while the entire source base is TypeScript — structural validation scripts that enforce TypeScript architectural rules are themselves exempt from TypeScript's type checking, creating an ironic enforcement gap where the enforcer cannot be enforced.
[anti-pattern] [info] No shared utility module exists across the 4 scripts; as the script count grows, ad-hoc duplication of filesystem traversal and exit-code patterns will accumulate. Extract a shared scripts/util.mjs when a third script reuses the same pattern.
[suggestion] [info] Naming inconsistency: three scripts use verb-object form ('check-gateway-regex', 'check-gateway-test', 'test-zone-consistency') but use two different verb prefixes ('check-' vs 'test-'). Standardize on one prefix (e.g. 'check-') to make the script inventory self-documenting and prevent the next script from introducing a third convention.
[suggestion] [info] hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs is named after a specific package while check-gateway-*.mjs and test-zone-consistency.mjs are package-agnostic. Either rename it to reflect a cross-package concern (e.g. callgraph-analysis.mjs) or move it into packages/hench/ as a package-local dev script. The current placement conflates package-scoped introspection with monorepo-wide enforcement.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "Monorepo Maintenance Scripts" (monorepo-maintenance-scripts) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds

</findings>

<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 4 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Scripts enforce gateway import discipline (check-gateway-regex.mjs, check-gateway-test.mjs) and zone consistency, acting as lightweight CI guards outside the main test suite.
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs suggests ongoing architectural introspection tooling — keeping this in a dedicated scripts/ dir makes it easy to run ad-hoc without polluting package source.
- Zero cohesion and zero coupling are expected for a collection of standalone scripts; this is not a quality concern but confirms these scripts have no shared infrastructure — if they grow, a shared utility module could reduce duplication.
- Cohesion of 0 is expected for standalone scripts with no inter-file imports; no action needed unless the script count grows significantly.
- Gateway validation scripts (check-gateway-regex.mjs, check-gateway-test.mjs) duplicate enforcement that could live in CI — confirm these are wired into the CI pipeline to guarantee they run on every PR.
- Scripts use .mjs extension while most source is TypeScript — consider whether type-safe TypeScript scripts would catch more issues at author-time.
- Zero coupling from this zone to any other zone means gateway validation scripts can be safely run at any pipeline stage without requiring a build step or cross-package resolution — a practical advantage for pre-commit hooks.
- The scripts zone validates the gateway pattern structurally (regex + test checks) but has no visibility into runtime behavior — it is a static linter, not a behavioral guard, so gateway regressions that pass the pattern check but fail at runtime will only be caught by e2e tests.
- monorepo-maintenance-scripts enforces gateway discipline for hench and web but has no equivalent check for the rex or sourcevision packages, leaving those gateways unguarded by static script validation.
- Scripts zone is a pure leaf in the dependency graph (coupling 0, no imports from other zones) — this property should be preserved; any future shared utility pulled in from a package would create an implicit build-time dependency on that package.
- Scripts are .mjs (not TypeScript) so they cannot be typechecked by tsc — type errors or API mismatches in script logic are only caught at runtime, not at author-time; this is the weakest static safety guarantee of any enforcement layer in the monorepo.
- The four maintenance scripts share no imports but likely share common patterns (file reading, regex matching, exit-code reporting) — if a fifth script is added without extracting shared utilities, copy-paste errors in exit-code handling or error reporting will accumulate silently.
- Maintenance scripts use .mjs (untyped) while the entire source base is TypeScript — structural validation scripts that enforce TypeScript architectural rules are themselves exempt from TypeScript's type checking, creating an ironic enforcement gap where the enforcer cannot be enforced.
- No shared utility module exists across the 4 scripts; as the script count grows, ad-hoc duplication of filesystem traversal and exit-code patterns will accumulate. Extract a shared scripts/util.mjs when a third script reuses the same pattern.
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs is package-scoped (hench-specific) while its three peers are cross-cutting architectural tools — the naming and scope are inconsistent with the zone's purpose as a monorepo-wide enforcement layer.
- Script naming uses two different verb prefixes without a documented convention: check-gateway-regex.mjs and check-gateway-test.mjs use 'check-' while test-zone-consistency.mjs uses 'test-' — within a four-file zone this inconsistency is a signal of independent authorship rather than a deliberate naming system.
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs is named after a specific package while check-gateway-*.mjs and test-zone-consistency.mjs are package-agnostic. Either rename it to reflect a cross-package concern (e.g. callgraph-analysis.mjs) or move it into packages/hench/ as a package-local dev script. The current placement conflates package-scoped introspection with monorepo-wide enforcement.
- Naming inconsistency: three scripts use verb-object form ('check-gateway-regex', 'check-gateway-test', 'test-zone-consistency') but use two different verb prefixes ('check-' vs 'test-'). Standardize on one prefix (e.g. 'check-') to make the script inventory self-documenting and prevent the next script from introducing a third convention.
- [call graph] 6 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)
